text:
I'm very interested in new graph layout ideas.

One idea I had was to show the same infinite canvas in a projective 3D view, so that the user can use WASD keys to navigate the graph like games. Nodes would be buildings, edges would be roads. Nested nodes would require entering the building etc. Perhaps it would invoke humans' ability to memorize things spatially better than plain 2D canvas.

text: In code flow canvas I dont have auto-graph layout yet (but it is on my wish-list).. I do have (limited) keyboard navigation with the tab-key though

elements.text: In code flow canvas I dont have auto-graph layout yet (but it is on my wish-list).. I do have (limited) keyboard navigation with the tab-key though

text: Layout algorithms also need to take z-position into account. For eg, child nodes should be drawn _on top of_ the parent node, to ensure that they are always visible. I ran into a bug yesterday when I added a keyboard shortcut for inserting a parent in the parent hierarchy.

the parent node, to ensure that they are always visible. I ran into a bug yesterday when I added a keyboard shortcut for inserting a parent in the parent hierarchy.

text: what I liked about the grids is they had a fairly straight forward objective function which is extensible <https://userinterfaces.aalto.fi/grids/resources/GRIDS-Formulation.pdf>
